thag9.1:1.3: comprehending stress, the wise man does jhāna, mindful,
thag9.1:1.4: there’s no greater enjoyment than that.
thag9.1:2.1: When, striking down attachment, the bringer of stress
thag9.1:2.2: & craving, the bringer of stress bound up with objectification,
thag9.1:2.4: there’s no greater enjoyment than that.
thag9.1:3.1: When, touching with discernment the auspicious, two-times-four road,
thag9.1:3.2: the ultimate path, cleansing away all defilement,
thag9.1:3.4: there’s no greater enjoyment than that.
thag9.1:4.1: When he develops the peaceful state,
thag9.1:4.3: cleansing away all defilement, cutting through fetters & bonds—
thag9.1:4.4: there’s no greater enjoyment than that.
thag9.1:5.1: When, in the sky, the thundercloud roars,
thag9.1:5.2: with torrents of rain on all sides of the path of the birds,
thag9.1:5.3: and the monk, having gone to a cave, does jhāna,
thag9.1:5.4: there’s no greater enjoyment than that.
thag9.1:6.1: When, seated on the bank of rivers covered with flowers,
thag9.1:6.3: happy at heart, he does jhāna,
thag9.1:6.4: there’s no greater enjoyment than that.
thag9.1:7.1: When, at midnight in the secluded grove,
thag9.1:7.2: the devas drizzle, the fanged animals roar,
thag9.1:7.3: and the monk, having gone to a cave, does jhāna,
thag9.1:7.4: there’s no greater enjoyment than that.
thag9.1:8.1: When, having kept his own thoughts in check,
thag9.1:8.2: taking refuge in the mountains in a mountain fissure,
thag9.1:8.3: free from disturbance, free from barrenness, he does jhāna,
thag9.1:8.4: there’s no greater enjoyment than that.
thag9.1:9.1: When, happy, destroying stain, barrenness, grief,
thag9.1:9.2: free from door-bolts, free from underbrush, free from arrows,
thag9.1:9.3: having put an end to all effluents, he does jhāna,
thag9.1:9.4: there’s no greater enjoyment than that.
